name: Castillo de Burgalimar and viewpoint to the Rumblar reservoir - gravel loop from Guarromán
name_original: Castillo de Burgalimar y mirador al pantano del Rumblar - bucle gravel desde Guarromán
region_intro: Touring cycling around Guarromán, located in Jaén province, Andalusia, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor exploration. The region features varied terrain, from a central urban area to more elevated and rugged sectors in the northwest and southeast. Key natural features include the Rumblar River and the Rumblar Reservoir, which traverse undulating terrain characteristic of the Sierra Morena mountains. This topographical diversity provides a range of routes suitable for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Guarromán The most popular touring cycling route is Ibero-Roman city of Castulo - circular via the Linares Greenway, a 16.0 miles (25.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 1 minute to complete. This route offers a historical and scenic ride to an ancient Ibero-Roman city. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Castillo de Burgalimar and viewpoint to the Rumblar reservoir - gravel loop from Guarromán, a moderate 20.4 miles (32.9 km) path. This gravel route combines natural beauty with a historic site and reservoir views. Local touring cyclists also love the Sanctuary of the Virgin of Linarejos – Chimney of the Cruz loop from Antigua Estación de Almería-San José, a 20.2 miles (32.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Touring cycling around Guarromán is defined by the rolling hills of the Sierra Morena, the Rumblar River, and diverse terrain that transitions from elevated sectors to more gentle paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to more challenging routes. The routes in Guarromán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 900 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Guarromán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Guarromán?
A: There are over 75 touring cycling routes around Guarromán, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes over 900 times, highlighting the region's appeal for cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in Guarromán?
A: Guarromán's terrain is quite diverse, ranging from a more central urban area to elevated and rugged sectors in the northwest and southeast. You'll encounter the rolling hills characteristic of the Sierra Morena mountains, with routes traversing undulating paths along the Rumblar River and its reservoir. This variety means you can find both gentler paths and more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Guarromán offers several easier routes. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are at least 3 routes classified as easy. These typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, making them suitable for a more relaxed ride or for families cycling together.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes around Guarromán are circular, allowing you to start and end your journey in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996438432/ibero-roman-city-of-castulo-circular-via-the-linares-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibero-Roman city of Castulo - circular via the Linares Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a historical and scenic ride.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes around Guarromán offer a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You can cycle past the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4008210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rumblar Reservoir Dam&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rumblar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; itself. Historical highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6700562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Cristo del Llano&lt;/a&gt;, a 16th-17th century hermitage, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Linarejos&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996302354/castillo-de-burgalimar-and-viewpoint-to-the-rumblar-reservoir-gravel-loop-from-guarroman" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Burgalimar and viewpoint to the Rumblar reservoir - gravel loop from Guarromán&lt;/a&gt; route specifically combines natural beauty with a historic site.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Guarromán?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, which transition from mountainous areas to more gentle paths, and the opportunity to combine natural exploration with visits to historical sites like the Ibero-Roman city of Castulo.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through towns or offer cultural stops?
A: Yes, several routes connect to nearby towns and cultural points of interest. For instance, you can find routes that lead to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Linares&lt;/a&gt;, offering stops at places like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38229534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Toros de Linares&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38232688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Linarejos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Guarromán?
A: Andalusia, where Guarromán is located, is celebrated for its favorable weather conditions for cycle touring throughout the year. While specific recommendations depend on personal preference, the mild winters and warm springs/autumns generally offer comfortable cycling temperatures, allowing you to enjoy the diverse landscapes without extreme heat.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. While there are easy and moderate options, a significant portion of the routes around Guarromán, specifically 54 out of 77, are classified as difficult. These routes typically feature greater distances and more substantial elevation gains, providing a good challenge for experienced touring cyclists looking for a demanding ride through the Sierra Morena.

Q: Can I find scenic viewpoints along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the varied topography of Guarromán and its integration into the Sierra Morena comarca provide numerous opportunities for scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996302354/castillo-de-burgalimar-and-viewpoint-to-the-rumblar-reservoir-gravel-loop-from-guarroman" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Burgalimar and viewpoint to the Rumblar reservoir - gravel loop from Guarromán&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, is specifically highlighted for its reservoir views. You can also find the dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Rumblar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; highlight.

Q: Are there any routes that are particularly long for extended touring?
A: While many routes are suitable for day trips, the region's accessibility to broader networks like the 'TransAndalus' and 'Transnevada' suggests that longer, multi-day touring opportunities are available for those looking to explore beyond Guarromán's immediate vicinity. Within the local routes, you'll find options up to 32.9 km, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996302354/castillo-de-burgalimar-and-viewpoint-to-the-rumblar-reservoir-gravel-loop-from-guarroman" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Burgalimar and viewpoint to the Rumblar reservoir - gravel loop from Guarromán&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some unique natural features to look out for while cycling?
A: The region is defined by the Rumblar River and the prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4008210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rumblar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which are central to many routes. You'll also experience the expansive mountain scenery of the Sierra Morena. The 'smell of the mountains,' attributed to the cistus plants, adds a unique sensory element to your ride.
tips: Peñalosa is an Argaric archaeological site located in the municipality of Baños de la Encina, in the province of Jaén, Spain. It is located on a hill that controls the old valley of the Rumblar River, currently partially flooded by the waters of the homonymous dam.
Also known as Burgalimar Castle, it is a magnificent example of Andalusian military architecture from the Caliphate period, as its structure has hardly suffered any modifications or damage.

The Rumblar reservoir built in 1941 in the course of the Rumblar river, in the municipality of Baños de la Encina.

The main water supplies are the Rumblar river itself, the Murquigüelo stream, the Higueruelas stream, and the Chico or Renegadero river.

Romanesque sanctuary dating from the 13th century that was reformed in the 17th century.
Built in ashlar stone with a barrel vault, high choir, screen, and seven side altars.

waypoints.name: Castle of Baños de la Encina (Burgalimar Castle)
Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Encina
